meddpiccNotes.metrics: VP Ops estimated 20% reduction in cross-team handoff delays.
meddpiccNotes.economicBuyer: VP Operations described as 'the one who signs off on tools under $75k' — treated as confirmed economic buyer in CRM notes.
meddpiccNotes.decisionCriteria: Integration with existing PM tool, SOC2, sub-$45k annual budget explicitly stated by VP Ops on 2026-02-18 call.
meddpiccNotes.decisionProcess: VP Ops approves directly; no further approval layer mentioned.
meddpiccNotes.paperProcess: Standard MSA, no unusual redlines expected per VP Ops.
meddpiccNotes.identifyPain: Cross-team handoffs were slipping SLAs by 2-3 days on average.
meddpiccNotes.champion: VP Operations drove every call, was highly engaged through mid-February, then stopped responding after the proposal was sent.
meddpiccNotes.competition: Rippling Workflows named once, early, as 'something we're also looking at.'

activityTimeline.summary: Product demo, VP Operations very engaged, asked detailed integration questions.
Budget discussion. VP Operations states budget ceiling is $45,000/year.
VP Operations acknowledges receiving proposal, says will review internally.

emails.subject: Re: Budget for FY planning
emails.snippet: Just to confirm from our call — anything we bring on this cycle needs to land under $45k annually. Above that and I have to escalate.

emails.subject: Re: Proposal — Workflow Automation
emails.snippet: Got the proposal, thanks. Will review internally and get back to you.

proposal.summary: Proposal priced at $58,000/year — above the $45,000 ceiling VP Operations stated two weeks earlier. No note in CRM flagging the mismatch before sending.
proposal.scopeSummary: Full workflow automation suite, unlimited seats, premium support tier.
